entries.title: back
entries.body: one haves to imagine sisyphus as someone that loves making and remaking his website. been up to good here and there, look at my blogpost. also ai is a thing now ill have to get used to it

entries.title: back
entries.body: happy yearmas, website is back after significant downtime, been to a few hackercamps, learning and having fun, venezuela is free, many such cases.

entries.title: til
entries.body: wintegreen plant is not the same as wintergreen flavour, which is done with peppermint: this confused me a lot.

entries.title: 302 found
entries.body:
i had been looking for a third place for so long and i finally found it, it is right now.
(my third place has an API and that makes it special)

entries.title: three things i made
entries.body:
i've been bored out of my mind recently, so i have been making things, dumb things, things that have already been made by people before in way higher quality, but i just need a way to pass the time. n1: i made a calculator in minecraft! it ain't pretty, it ain't optimized, but it works! and the best part is that i made it using only my understanding of logic gates so it actually feels like a product of my imagination even though "minecaft calculator" is a concept that has been explored since the game was born.

n2: i made some dumb "visual art" during class (if you can even call it that) after vaguely learning what a vanishing point is, as always i made it on processing, if i explored the concept more it could become vaguely reminiscent of polyfauna?.

nickyis.online/assets/cooldots.rar
n3: conways game of life on a launchpad, this is by far the dumbest of the bunch but it came to me on a vision and i had to make it true, the code is disgusting because of how janky the libraries for the launchpad are so i wont be sharing it but hey! it works!

so these are three things i have made this year. i hope i can make many more! i also discovered emails are cool and if you're reading this you should send me one!(also two great pieces of media that i have been consuming: paper mario for the nintendo wii and severance the tv show by ben stiller)
website statistics!, they work amazingly here

entries.title: sinwaves are cool!
entries.body: playing with grafical user interfaces, i really like the oscilloscope aestethic, i'd love to make an actual working oscilloscope w/ an arduino or something if that even is possible : sinwave.rar (windows executable)

entries.title: this is a test
entries.body: made a script that formats blog entries for me -- now im testing it!★

entries.title: PSA
entries.body: Public Service Announcement: if you wish to delete all of the contents in a folder the correct syntax is NOT "user@computer:~/folder$ sudo rm -rf /*", the correct syntax IS "user@computer:~/folder$ sudo rm -rf ./*" : this mistake ruined my weekend, thank god for backups★

entries.title: rome and computers
entries.body: yesterday my trusty macbook, that has carried me gracefully through the 'triennio' with no issues (except for a broken battery and a broken trackpad ribbon cable that i had to replace, and an infinity of software problems caused by how despicable macOS can be at times) oficially died. i took it out of my backpack and out of nowhere the screen just.. like.. stopped working, so i inmediately did what any sane person would do and bought a lenovo thinkpad: that ought to last. -- also i'm going to rome!, so i hope i will get to add some new photos to the site gallery.★

entries.title: languages
entries.body: i find it so cute the way the french and dutch both say "you're welcome" in long ways and then abbreviate it, S.V.P and A.U.B is such an adorable thing to read // also i am currently working on a radio online receiver: i find fm radio as a concept hilarious and i NEED this website to relay some generic italian pop music fm station.★

entries.title: webrings
beginning
entries.body: i worked on this website during two entire school days, and i've been working on the "server" (30€ computer) that holds it for about two months, i'm very happy on how it all turned out even if there's some things that are not very efficient, but i think i still did a good job and the site looks and feels exactly how i wanted it(even on mobile!)★
